$schema_note: Acknowledged security findings. DeepSeek workflow skips these when counting CRITICAL/HIGH blockers. Each entry has a title pattern (case-insensitive substring match) and explanation.

acknowledged.reason: MITIGATED: MAX_WORKER_THREADS=256 cap + thread::Builder (no panic on spawn fail)
MITIGATED: MAX_DNS_RESOLVER_THREADS=4, 3s timeout, slot cleanup on spawn failure
MITIGATED: same as DNS Resolver — bounded pool with timeout
MITIGATED: global_orphan_byte_limit() + per-block size check + checked_add at insertion
MITIGATED: same as Orphan Block Pool — byte limit + eviction + size check
MITIGATED: checked_add used everywhere, saturating_add only for non-overflow-sensitive counters
MITIGATED: orphan pool operations protected by mutex, no concurrent access to byte counter. Covers: 'race condition in global orphan', 'non-atomic update of global orphan'.
MITIGATED: non-devnet requires explicit --genesis-file + handshake validates genesis_hash (Go parity)
MITIGATED: compact_size parsing enforces MAX_INV_ITEMS before allocation
ACCEPTED_RISK: lock held during message processing bounded by MAX_RELAY_MSG_BYTES + block validation. Splitting requires major async refactor — deferred to post-MVP.
MITIGATED: OUTBOUND_SLOT_RESERVE=2 prevents inbound from starving outbound dials

acknowledged.title_pattern: Transaction ID Malleability Due to Non-Canonical Parsing
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: matches the exact DeepSeek title. Go canonicalTxID (handlers_tx.go:41) uses identical consumed!=len check. Zero divergence — both reject trailing bytes.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Consensus Divergence Risk in Transaction ID Calculation
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: matches the exact earlier DeepSeek title for the same parser concern. Rust canonical_txid mirrors Go canonicalTxID exactly (consumed != len → error).
ACCEPTED_RISK: Go parity — Go also has no per-peer send queue limit. Each frame ~70 bytes (INV). Real risk LOW at current throughput. Follow-up: add per-peer cap.
FALSE_POSITIVE: Go handleTx (handlers_tx.go:9-34) also does NOT submit to consensus mempool — only relay pool + re-announce. Mempool admission is a separate RPC path.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Invalid Transactions Stored in Relay Pool
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: same Go-parity rationale as Transaction Relay Without Validation — the relay pool is intentionally separate from consensus mempool admission on both Rust and Go paths.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Network transactions not submitted to mempool
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: Go parity — network tx relay does not imply consensus mempool admission. Rust mirrors Go by relaying through the relay pool and keeping mempool admission as a separate path.

FALSE_POSITIVE: Go parity — relay pool storage is intentionally decoupled from consensus mempool validation, so this is the same reviewed false-positive family as Transaction Relay Without Validation.
ACCEPTED_RISK: Go parity — Go MemoryTxPool also count-based (1000) without byte limit. MAX_RELAY_MSG_BYTES limits individual tx size.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Unbounded Relay Pool Growth Under Attack
acknowledged.reason: ACCEPTED_RISK: same reviewed risk family as Memory Exhaustion via Large Transactions — Go parity, bounded entry count, and MAX_RELAY_MSG_BYTES cap keep this as a non-blocking follow-up rather than a merge blocker.
ACCEPTED_RISK: poison = thread panic = node already degraded. Returning error is correct — no recovery possible from panicked thread.
MITIGATED: MAX_GETDATA_RESPONSE_BYTES (128MB) + MAX_GETDATA_RESPONSE_BLOCKS (16) cap. Pre-existing design, not new in this PR.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Mempool Not Updated with P2P Transactions
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: Go parity — P2P relay plumbing is separate from consensus mempool accounting. The reviewed claim conflates relay propagation with mempool lifecycle management.
FALSE_POSITIVE: same Go-parity rationale as the tx_relay mempool comments — P2P relay intentionally does not imply immediate consensus mempool admission.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Fee=0 for RPC Transactions Causes Priority Distortion
acknowledged.reason: ACCEPTED_RISK: Go relay also uses relay-specific metadata, not consensus fee. fee=0 for RPC-submitted txs matches Go where RelayMetadata is separate from mempool fee.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Fee=0 for RPC Transactions Causes Priority Distortion
acknowledged.reason: ACCEPTED_RISK: same reviewed relay-metadata tradeoff as the tx_relay finding — Go parity keeps relay metadata separate from consensus fee accounting.
ACCEPTED_RISK: O(n) worst-tx selection in relay_pool matches Go implementation and is bounded by the 1000-entry relay pool cap. Not a blocker.
ACCEPTED_RISK: tx_seen eviction strategy matches Go implementation; worst-case scan is bounded by the fixed seen-set capacity and is not a merge blocker.
ACCEPTED_RISK: single parse per tx relay path. Optimizing to reuse parsed tx is premature — relay is not hot path.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Worker Pool Hanging Causes Node Denial-of-Service
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: Go parity — go/consensus/worker_pool.go is the same cooperative primitive with no per-task kill switch. Tasks are internal consensus closures, not attacker-supplied code; adding forceful timeouts would change semantics and is not implementable safely in std threads.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Unbounded Memory Consumption in Results Buffer
acknowledged.reason: ACCEPTED_RISK: Go parity — ordered reduction requires one result slot per submitted task just like Go make([]WorkerResult, n). The caller already materializes the task batch; concrete task-count bounds belong at future block-level integration sites, not this infrastructure primitive.

acknowledged.title_pattern: worker pool error reporting loses transaction index
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: run_worker_pool returns results in submission order (deterministic). PTCs are in tx_index order from precompute. first_tx_error iterates in this order, so first-by-iteration ≡ first-by-tx_index. tx_index=0 impossible (coinbase excluded).

FALSE_POSITIVE: ptc.resolved_inputs never contains zero-witness covenant types (anchor/da_commit excluded by precompute). All covenant types reaching the loop have witness_slots >= 1. sig_count tracks validated inputs (Go parity), not individual sig verifications.

FALSE_POSITIVE: witness_cursor <= witness_end <= tx.witness.len() bounded by block weight limit. slots from witness_slots() max 255 (u8). On 64-bit (RUBIN requirement) overflow impossible. Block size limits prevent >4B witness items even on 32-bit.

FALSE_POSITIVE: Go already has queued/deferred signature validation in the parallel block path. Rust deferred helper is parity scaffolding, not a new Rust-only acceptance rule. Structural errors still fail inline; signature errors are only deferred to flush within the same tx/block validation boundary.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Vault whitelist check before signature flush
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: Go parallel vault validation already queues threshold signatures before whitelist checks and flushes later at the block validation boundary. Rust mirrors that ordering, so this is not a Rust-only divergence.

FALSE_POSITIVE: SigCheckQueue::ensure_registry compares SuiteRegistry by value (PartialEq/Eq), not pointer identity. The queue is tx-local on the live path; there is no concurrent cross-registry mutation surface here.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Unbounded queue growth via usize overflow
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: queue byte accounting uses checked_add in both sigcheck_task_bytes() and next_queued_bytes(); overflow fails closed with TxErrWitnessOverflow instead of bypassing the budget.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Inconsistent Error Handling in Native Path
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE for Rust-vs-Go parity: Go already keeps registry-native suites on the native queue path and non-native verify_sig_ext suites inline. Rust mirrors that split; this is not a new divergence.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Consensus Divergence Risk Due to Multiple DA Commit Outputs
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: Go parity — CollectDAPayloadCommitTasks also scans outputs, takes the first COV_TYPE_DA_COMMIT entry with 32-byte covenant data, and breaks. Structural DA-set integrity is enforced earlier in validateDASetIntegrity / validate_block_basic before this helper is reachable.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Undefined ML-DSA constants risk consensus divergence masking
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: ML_DSA_87_PUBKEY_BYTES (2592), ML_DSA_87_SIG_BYTES (4627), SUITE_ID_ML_DSA_87 are exported constants defined in constants.go:66+ of the same consensus package. Test file is in-package (_test.go) with direct access.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Missing panic handling in DA chunk fuzz target
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: cargo-fuzz/libfuzzer catches panics via Rust panic hook automatically. catch_unwind in fuzz targets is an anti-pattern that hides real bugs instead of exposing them.
ADDRESSED: hash module is private (mod hash). Fuzz target mirrors rubin_consensus::hash::sha3_256 using same sha3 crate and algorithm.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Consensus divergence in UTXO state handling
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: Fuzz test UTXO map has exactly 1 entry — iteration order irrelevant. Determinism check compares two calls of same Go code, not Go vs Rust. Production PostStateDigest uses sorted iteration.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Signature validation bypass in fuzz test
acknowledged.reason: FALSE_POSITIVE: ML_DSA_87_SIG_BYTES+1 (4628) is standard wire format: 4627-byte signature + 1 sighash type byte. Production block_basic.go:507 validates this exact length. Used in all existing tests.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Consensus Divergence Risk in UTXO State Representation
acknowledged.reason: FALSE POSITIVE: finding is in fuzz test code, not production consensus logic. Fuzz targets intentionally construct minimal state representations to exercise error paths.
FALSE POSITIVE: fuzz test uses real ML-DSA-87 signatures via test keypair; signature structure is dictated by consensus signing API.
FALSE POSITIVE: local sha3_256 mirrors rubin_consensus::hash::sha3_256 (private module). Both use identical sha3 crate. No divergence risk.

acknowledged.title_pattern: UTXO state divergence risk between Go and Rust
acknowledged.reason: FALSE POSITIVE: Go big.Int and Rust u128 are both overflow-safe for AlreadyGenerated. Go checks overflow explicitly (connect_block_inmem.go:198-202), Rust uses checked_add (connect_block_inmem.rs:183). Max subsidy bounded by spec; u128 sufficient. Architectural decision, not a bug.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Missing ML-DSA-87 signature verification in fuzz test
acknowledged.reason: FALSE POSITIVE: ApplyNonCoinbaseTxBasicUpdate internally verifies ML-DSA-87 signatures through the full consensus validation path. Redundant external VerifyDigest32 would test the signing API, not the consensus pipeline.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Inconsistent worker=0 handling in DA verification
acknowledged.reason: FALSE POSITIVE: finding references test file outside PR diff. Go worker_pool.go:84-88 falls back to GOMAXPROCS(0) for workers<=0. Rust worker pool has equivalent fallback. Not a consensus divergence.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Insufficient input validation in block connection fuzz
acknowledged.reason: FALSE POSITIVE: fuzz targets intentionally feed arbitrary bytes including sub-header-size inputs. Adding minimum header check would REDUCE fuzz coverage by excluding deserialization error paths that must not panic.

acknowledged.title_pattern: Inconsistent fuzz input structures between Go and Rust
acknowledged.reason: FALSE POSITIVE: Go testing.F supports multiple corpus entries natively; libfuzzer only supports single byte slice. Input structure difference is a platform limitation, not a test quality issue. Both achieve equivalent coverage through different decomposition strategies.
